sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for NES action-platformer

special_mechanics.name: Backward Flip Jump
special_mechanics.description: Ken can perform a neutral upward jump and then press the directional pad opposite to his facing direction to execute a backward flip jump. This enables downward projectile shots when pressing B mid-air.

special_mechanics.name: Wall Climb
special_mechanics.description: Ken can climb walls and pole-like structures throughout stages.

special_mechanics.name: Ledge Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: Ken can hang onto and climb (or drop down from) certain kinds of ledges.

special_mechanics.name: Projectile System
special_mechanics.description: Ken launches energy projectiles with fists: straight horizontal, upward vertical (d-pad up + B), curved power shots (d-pad left/right + B with greater range/power), and diagonal upward kicks (d-pad down + B). Requires flip jump to shoot downward.

physics_values.projectile_range_scaling: increases with power-up capsules (up to 5 levels)

special_mechanics.name: Flip Shield Capsule
special_mechanics.description: Power-up that allows player to damage enemies during flip jumps.

tier_notes: Tier 3 (empirical/qualitative) - No precise physics parameters documented in available sources. Sources contain narrative/design information but lack quantitative movement data. Game features directional mid-air control capability and multi-directional projectile mechanics typical of NES-era action platformers.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Street Fighter 2010: The Final Fight (regional differences and gameplay mechanics)
Wikipedia - Street Fighter 2010: The Final Fight (gameplay section)
